behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily retry/backoff math (attempt index + cap). The real failure boundary is local logic, not module wiring or I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Test the backoff calculator (or retry-policy function) directly with attempt counts 1..N and a max cap; assert exponential progression and cap enforcement.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure often appears at the handoff between HTTP response parsing/classification and retry policy (e.g., status/header mapping mismatch). Unit tests on either piece alone can miss this boundary bug.
behaviors.test_description: Wire client + response classifier + retry policy with a stub transport returning a 429 (and relevant headers). Assert the client classifies it as rate-limit and schedules the expected delay.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: This depends on coordination between timer/backoff, request replay, and response handling. The bug is in module interaction, not isolated logic.
behaviors.test_description: Using fake timers and a stub transport, make first call return rate-limited and second call succeed; advance time and assert the original request is retried with same payload and the caller receives the success value.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed QuotaExhaustedError rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: The typed error is usually produced after retry-loop exhaustion and surfaced via the public API. That mapping can fail at component boundaries even if local constructors are correct.
behaviors.test_description: Stub transport to always return rate-limit, run through max retries with fake timers, and assert rejection is specifically QuotaExhaustedError (type and key fields), not a generic Error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end control-flow property across multiple local modules (retry engine, quota state, scheduler). Unit tests cannot prove loop termination across the chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real in-process retry stack with a transport that keeps returning rate-limit, use fake timers to drive all waits, and assert completion within bounded attempts/time with final QuotaExhaustedError.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is shared mutable state across concurrent callers and request orchestration. This is specifically a multi-module interaction problem.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client, fire concurrent call() invocations, and use a deterministic stub transport/quota limiter to assert all callers consume a single shared quota state (not per-call isolated counters).

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: FIFO dispatch requires queue + scheduler + send path to work together under async timing. Single-function tests can miss ordering regressions caused by interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Queue multiple calls under throttled conditions, advance fake timers, and assert transport send order matches submission order exactly.
